steps.title: Identify what nothing can overcome
steps.content: The construction “there is little that can _____ being the first to do something” is the key signal. The phrase “little that can” tells us the blank must express something that nothing can do to being first—it must mean overcome, surpass, or match the power of being first.

steps.title: Apply the choice to the context
steps.content:
“Prevail over” fills the blank to give: “There is little that can prevail over being the first to do something.” This means almost nothing can surpass the significance of being first—which is exactly why the passage adds the example: people always remember the first, since nothing else can compete for that place in memory. The other three choices produce illogical meanings or don't express this overcome relationship.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
